text: You broke the ice, asked open questions, and agreed to keep in touch.
Today, inside the meeting, the right tone comes from can, could, and should.

Thanks for joining. Let's start. The agenda has three items today. Could we begin with the contract?
Of course. Can I raise a point first? I think we should discuss the deadline early.
Good idea. Could you recap where we are with the cross-border contract?
Sure. We are close, but we should postpone the signing until the review is done.
Agreed. Let's note that as an action item. Could you take the minutes today?
I can do that. Should we move on to the next item now?
Could you recap the main points?
We should postpone this to next week.
Could you take the minutes today?
Let's move on to the next item.

Hi Simone, this is Daniel. I am calling about our project meeting on Thursday. I will chair the meeting, and I am sending the agenda this afternoon. There are three items on it. First, could you raise the point about the cross-border contract? It is important, so we should discuss it early. Second, we need to decide on the new deadline. If we run out of time, we can postpone the last item to next week. Could you also take the minutes this time? After the meeting, I will send the action items to everyone. If you have anything to add to the agenda, please let me know before Thursday. Thanks, and talk soon.

Hi team, this is Simone with a quick recap of today's project meeting. Daniel chaired it, and we followed the agenda closely. First, I raised the point about the cross-border contract, and we agreed it is the priority. Then we discussed the new deadline. We did not have time for the last item, so we decided to postpone it to next Thursday. I took the minutes, and I will share them this afternoon. There are two action items for our team. I should review the contract by Friday, and you should send your questions before the next meeting. If anyone wants to add something to the agenda, please let me know. Thanks for your great work, and let us move on with confidence.

Daniel opens the meeting and shares the agenda. Simone raises the point about the cross-border contract first. The team discusses the new deadline and agrees on a plan. They run out of time, so they postpone the last item to next week. Simone takes the minutes and sends the action items to everyone.
